name: HubSpot_CRM_Navigator
description: Provides intelligent read-only access to HubSpot CRM data including contacts, deals, companies, and pipeline information. Bridges the Mind's business intelligence needs to actionable customer relationship data for sales enablement and CRM analysis.
progressive_unlock: Contact and company searching available immediately. Deal pipeline and detailed deal information unlock after Mind has searched contacts at least once to establish CRM familiarity and usage patterns.
constraints: READ-ONLY access: Never attempt to create, update, or delete contacts, deals, or companies
Respect HubSpot rate limit of 100 requests per 10 seconds for private apps
Always use property selection parameter to request only necessary fields and reduce bandwidth
Never expose HubSpot API token in logs, outputs, or error messages
Pagination: Always respect 'limit' parameter (default 10, max 100) and use 'after' cursor for large result sets
Cache search results in STREAM.entity for repeated lookups within 60-second window to optimize rate limits
Validate contact/deal/company IDs before making GET requests to avoid wasted API calls
For deal pipeline queries, cache pipeline structure in LTM with key 'hubspot_deal_pipeline' (24-hour TTL)
Return only requested properties to minimize response payload and improve efficiency
Maintain separate contact, company, and deal caches to support multi-entity queries without redundant API calls

steps.instruction: Validate input against Layer 3 Tool Schema. Confirm required parameters present, correctly typed, and within acceptable ranges (e.g., limit 1-100).
Retrieve HubSpot Private App token from TENET.apiKey.hubspot. Construct Authorization header as 'Bearer {token}'.

Determine query intent: contact search (email/name lookup), contact details (full profile), deal listing (pipeline view), deal details (specific deal analysis), company search (company research), or pipeline structure (sales process understanding).

For contact searches: execute HubSpot_SearchContacts with email, name, or property filters. Optimize with minimal required properties (firstname, lastname, email, phone, lifecyclestage). Cache results in STREAM.contact_search_{query_hash}.

For contact details: verify contact ID in STREAM cache or from previous search. Execute HubSpot_GetContact with associations=['deals', 'companies']. Store contact detail in STREAM.contact_{id} (60-second TTL).

For company searches: execute HubSpot_SearchCompanies with domain or name filters. Request properties like 'name', 'domain', 'industry', 'numberofemployees'. Cache in STREAM.company_search_{query_hash}.

For deal pipeline structure: check LTM for 'hubspot_deal_pipeline' cache (24-hour TTL). If not present, execute HubSpot_GetDealPipeline to retrieve all pipeline stages and store result in LTM. Use for deal stage context.

For deal listing: execute HubSpot_ListDeals with optional filters on dealstage or dealtype. Request essential properties (dealname, dealstage, closedate, amount, hubspot_owner_id). Implement pagination using 'limit' and 'after' for large result sets.

For deal details: verify deal ID and execute HubSpot_GetDeal with associations=['contacts', 'companies']. Include relevant properties (dealname, dealstage, closedate, amount, description). Store in STREAM.deal_{id} (60-second TTL).

Compile results respecting read-only constraint. Present contact/deal/company data with clear formatting. Include pipeline context when presenting deals. Update STREAM with query metrics (search count, result count) for analytics.
Log query execution in LTM with key 'hubspot_query_{timestamp}' including intent, tool used, result count, and execution time. Track rate limit usage (100 req/10s) to prevent throttling.
